namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addNode(observer);
    graph.addNode(target);

}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);

    // Get the singleton observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Record call count before reset
    int callCountBefore = observer->getCallCount();

    // Reset the observer node
    observer->reset();

    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->getCallCount() != callCountBefore) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age) 
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}
    
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }
    
    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }
    
    const reaction::Var<std::string>& getNameVar() const { return m_name; }
    const reaction::Var<int>& getAgeVar() const { return m_age; }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    // Create a reactive calculation that depends on name and age
    auto nameVar = person.getNameVar();
    auto ageVar = person.getAgeVar();
    auto info = reaction::calc(nameVar, ageVar, [](const std::string& name, int age) {
        return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
    });
    
    // Update the fields
    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    // Return the updated information
    return info.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <string>
#include <cmath>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    auto recomputeCount = reaction::var(0);
    auto lastValue = reaction::var(initialValue);

    // Define the filter function
    auto filter = [diffThreshold](double old_val, double new_val) {
        return std::abs(new_val - old_val) > diffThreshold;
    };

    // Define the calculation function
    auto calc_func = [&](double current_val) -> std::string {
        std::string status = (current_val > levelThreshold) ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
        lastValue = current_val;
        recomputeCount += 1;
        return status;
    };

    // Create the calculation with filter
    auto status_calc = reaction::calc(calc_func, sensor, filter);

    // Update sensor with first value
    sensor = firstUpdate;
    int count_after_first = recomputeCount.get();

    // Update sensor with second value
    sensor = secondUpdate;
    int count_after_second = recomputeCount.get();

    // Get final status
    std::string final_status = status_calc.get();

    return {final_status, count_after_first, count_after_second};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.registerThread();
    
    auto beforeCount = tm.getThreadCount();
    auto safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread worker([]() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread();
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));
    });
    
    worker.join();
    
    auto afterCount = tm.getThreadCount();
    auto safetyAfter = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore,
        safetyAfter,
        beforeCount,
        afterCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    auto recomputeCount = reaction::var(0);
    
    auto calculation = reaction::calc(
        [&]() { return reactiveVar.get() + deltaA + deltaB; },
        [&](int) { recomputeCount.set(recomputeCount.get() + 1); }
    );
    
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeCount.get();
    
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        reactiveVar.set(baseValue + deltaA);
        reactiveVar.set(baseValue + deltaA + deltaB);
    });
    
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeCount.get();
    int finalValue = calculation.get();
    
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        recomputeCountBeforeBatch,
        recomputeCountAfterBatch,
        finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variable
    auto reactVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Create recompute count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // Step 3: Create calculation
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return reactVar.get() + 1;
    });
    
    // Step 4: Create batch
    reaction::Batch batch;
    
    // Add operation to update the reactive variable
    batch.addOperation([&]() { reactVar.set(updatedValue); });
    
    // Step 5: Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // Step 6: Execute batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // Step 7: Record recompute count after execute
    int countAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    // Step 8: Close batch
    batch.close();
    
    // Step 9: Check if batch is closed after close
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    // Step 10: Ensure calculation is evaluated (recomputeCount should be 1)
    
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        closedInitially,
        closedAfterClose,
        countAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        reaction::ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    CacheProbeNode nodeA, nodeB, nodeC;
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);
    
    nodeA.addDirectObserver(nodeB);
    nodeB.addDirectObserver(nodeC);
    
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.getObservers(nodeC, observers);
    
    nodeC.valueChanged(true);
    
    auto statsBefore = graph.getCacheStats();
    size_t graphEntriesBefore = statsBefore.graphEntries;
    double hitRatioBefore = statsBefore.hitRatio;
    
    graph.triggerCacheCleanup();
    
    auto statsAfter = graph.getCacheStats();
    size_t graphEntriesAfter = statsAfter.graphEntries;
    double hitRatioAfter = statsAfter.hitRatio;
    
    nodeA.close();
    nodeB.close();
    nodeC.close();
    
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        graphEntriesBefore,
        graphEntriesAfter,
        hitRatioAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable with the initial value
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that uses the LastHandle policy
    auto calc = reaction::calc(source, [](int val) { return val; }, reaction::LastHandle{});
    
    // Get the initial result
    int initialResult = calc.get();
    
    // Update the source variable
    source.set(updatedValue);
    
    // Get the result after update
    int afterUpdate = calc.get();
    
    // Close the source node
    source.close();
    
    // Get the result after source closure (should retain last value due to LastHandle)
    int afterSourceClose = calc.get();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = afterUpdate,
        .afterSourceClose = afterSourceClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Lambda to create large payload with sequential data based on seed
    auto makeLargePayload = [largeSeed]() -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + static_cast<int>(i);
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Create small and large SBOResource instances
    reaction::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    reaction::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource(makeLargePayload());

    // Update small resource and check if value changed
    bool updateChanged = (updatedSmall != initialSmall);
    smallResource.update(SmallPayload{updatedSmall});

    // Retrieve storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();

    // Construct and return the status
    SboStorageStatus status;
    status.updateReportedChange = updateChanged;
    status.smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.usingSBO;
    status.smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stackUsage;
    status.largeInitialized = largeInfo.initialized;
    status.largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.usingSBO;

    return status;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    auto &tm = reaction::getThreadManager();
    tm.resetForTesting();

    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;
    SharedMutexResult result{};

    // Test without safety
    result.trySharedWithoutSafety = mutex.trySharedLock();
    if (result.trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard guard(mutex);
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.tryUniqueLock();
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }

    // Enable safety
    tm.enableSafety();

    // Test with safety
    result.trySharedWithSafety = mutex.trySharedLock();
    if (result.trySharedWithSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard guard(mutex);
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = mutex.tryUniqueLock();
    }

    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    tm.reset();
    
    // 3. Create a reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 4. Create a calculation
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&, var]() { return var.get() * multiplier; });
    
    // 5. Get value before reset
    int beforeResetValue = calc.get();
    
    // 6. Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety(true);
    
    // 7. Check if thread safety is enabled
    bool safetyEnabled = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset calculation logic
    calc.reset();
    
    // 9. Update source variable
    var.set(initialValue + 1);
    
    // 10. Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calc.get();
    
    // 11. Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        beforeResetValue,
        afterResetValue,
        safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with assigned names
    auto var1 = reaction::React::var(firstName);
    auto var2 = reaction::React::var(secondName);
    auto var3 = reaction::React::var(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into set to leverage React::operator< for ordering
    std::set<reaction::React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names from the set
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique (set size should be 3 if all unique)
    bool allUnique = reactSet.size() == 3;
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
        reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().notifyObservers(this, changed);
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    TrackingFieldNode owner("owner");
    TrackingFieldNode field("field");

    // Register nodes with observer graph
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().registerNode(&owner);
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().registerNode(&field);

    // Register field node with field graph under object ID
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.registerFieldNode(&field, objectId);

    // Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);

    // Trigger notification on field
    field.valueChanged(true);
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete = (owner.notifications() == 1);

    // Cleanup
    owner.clear();
    fieldGraph.clearDependencies(&owner);
    fieldGraph.removeObjectMappings(objectId);

    // Attempt to bind field to owner again
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);
    field.valueChanged(true);
    bool notifiedAfterDelete = (owner.notifications() == 1);

    // Close nodes
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().closeNode(&owner);
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().closeNode(&field);

    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBeforeDelete, notifiedAfterDelete};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackLimitSnapshot {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBeforeGuard;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfterGuard;
    bool templateAllocationAllowed;
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed;
    bool guardHeld;
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld;
};

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    auto& monitor = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Enable monitoring
    monitor.enable();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    monitor.initialize();
    
    // 3. Get usage statistics before guard
    auto statsBeforeGuard = monitor.getUsageStats();
    
    // 4. Check if template allocation is allowed
    bool templateAllocationAllowed = monitor.isTemplateAllocationAllowed();
    
    // 5. Check if runtime allocation is allowed  
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed = monitor.isRuntimeAllocationAllowed();
    
    // 6. Create large StackGuard instance
    constexpr size_t LARGE_STACK_SIZE = 1024 * 1024; // 1MB
    reaction::memory::StackGuard guard(LARGE_STACK_SIZE);
    
    // 7. Check if guard succeeded
    bool guardHeld = guard.succeeded();
    
    // 8. Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = false;
    if (guardHeld) {
        // Attempt to allocate a small chunk to test blocking
        void* testAllocation = monitor.allocate(1024);
        allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = (testAllocation == nullptr);
        
        // Free the test allocation if it was successful
        if (testAllocation != nullptr) {
            monitor.deallocate(testAllocation, 1024);
        }
    }
    
    // 9. Get usage statistics after guard
    auto statsAfterGuard = monitor.getUsageStats();
    
    // 10. Return stack limit snapshot
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBeforeGuard,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfterGuard,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllocationAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllocationAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardHeld,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {

    auto reactive_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto reactive_b = reaction::var(b);
    auto reactive_c = reaction::var(c);

    auto and_expr = reactive_a && reactive_b;
    auto or_expr = reactive_a || reactive_b;
    
    // Get initial results
    bool initialAnd = and_expr();
    bool initialOr = or_expr();
    
    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    reactive_a() = !a;
    reactive_b() = !b;
    
    // Get updated results
    bool updatedAnd = and_expr();
    bool updatedOr = or_expr();
    
    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {

        .hitCount = hitCount,
        .missCount = missCount,

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

template<typename Key, typename Value>
class IntCache : public CacheBase<Key, Value> {
public:
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::lookup;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::insert;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::cleanup;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::getStats;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    IntCache<int, int> cache;
    
    // Get initial cache statistics
    auto initialStats = cache.getStats();
    
    // Perform initial lookup (should be a miss)
    auto [firstValue, firstHit] = cache.lookup(42);
    bool firstLookupWasMiss = !firstHit;
    
    // Insert value into cache
    cache.insert(42, 100);
    
    // Perform second lookup (should be a hit)
    auto [secondValue, secondHit] = cache.lookup(42);
    bool secondLookupWasHit = secondHit;
    
    // Get cache statistics after insertion
    auto statsAfterInsert = cache.getStats();
    
    // Invalidate all cache entries and trigger cleanup
    cache.invalidateAll();
    cache.cleanup();
    
    // Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto statsAfterInvalidation = cache.getStats();
    
    // Calculate hit ratio
    std::size_t hitCount = statsAfterInvalidation.hits;
    std::size_t missCount = statsAfterInvalidation.misses;
    double hitRatio = (hitCount + missCount) > 0 ? 
                      static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount) : 0.0;
    
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = initialStats.entries,
        .entriesAfterInsert = statsAfterInsert.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalidation.entries,
        .hitCount = hitCount,
        .missCount = missCount,
        .hitRatio = hitRatio,
        .firstLookupWasMiss = firstLookupWasMiss,
        .secondLookupWasHit = secondLookupWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

But the actual implementation depends on the library's behavior. The function just collects the state, regardless of what the values are.

So the code should correctly call the necessary functions in order. The key points are:

- Use the singleton instance
- Reset the state
- Check safety before and after registrations
- Create a worker thread that also registers
- Properly join the thread
- Return the snapshot

I think that's all. The code should look like the above.

<answer>```cpp
#include <thread>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread worker([]() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });
    worker.join();
    
    bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{safetyBefore, safetyAfterMain, safetyAfterWorker};
}

} // namespace test_reaction

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
